metadata.notes: agent=yes (InvokeSubagents tool + /spawn; changelog 'Record parent session ID in persisted subagent sessions'); daemon=incapable — kiro records the parent link only in the .json sidecar (loop_id.agent_id.parent_id), never in the .jsonl the daemon tails, so no parent-child link is ever derived; frozen.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable (no kiro-cli parent-child linking path; the link lives only in the .json sidecar the adapter never reads)
driver_capability: n/a (frozen — no recording)

## Scenario

The parent session launches a foreground subagent; once the child is linked and its sidechain transcript is visible, the recipe SIGKILLs the parent's process group, leaving the child orphaned, and the daemon's pid_manager sweep must remove both rows with no spurious `working` transition for the orphan (issue #321 regression). The user-observable signal this asserts is `>= 1 child session linked to a parent` and `>= 1 subagent transcript` — i.e. the daemon must first SEE the child as a linked child session. That precondition is what fails for kiro-cli.

## Agent pillar (yes)

kiro-cli 2.5.1 ships subagents as a first-class capability. From the live binaries (`/opt/homebrew/bin/kiro-cli` 2.5.1 and `~/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat`):

- The chat binary embeds an `InvokeSubagents` tool — "Array of subagent invocations to execute in parallel" — plus the slash surface includes `/spawn` (FINDINGS.md §8) and a changelog line "Introducing Subagents - Delegate complex tasks to specialized agents with live progress tracking". So the agent genuinely spawns child agent sessions.
- A changelog string in the binary, "Record parent session ID in persisted subagent sessions", confirms kiro persists the parent→child link to disk. The live smoke session's `.json` sidecar exposes exactly that shape: `session_created_reason: "subagent"` and `session_state.conversation_metadata.user_turn_metadatas[].loop_id.agent_id.parent_id`. So `agent=yes` for spawning AND for persisting the link — the scenario's precondition (a child session linked to a parent) is producible by the agent.

## Daemon pillar (incapable)

The irrlicht daemon establishes a parent→child link by exactly the methods in `deriveParentSession` (`core/application/services/helpers.go:90`), plus a parser that sets `event.ParentSessionID`. NONE fire for kiro-cli:

1. Path pattern `.../<parent>/subagents/<child>.jsonl` — `deriveParentSessionID` (helpers.go:100). kiro session files are FLAT: `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl` with no `subagents/` directory (adapter Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli}`, agent.go:38), so this never matches.
2. Header `{"type":"session","parentSession":"..."}` — `deriveParentSessionFromTranscript` (helpers.go:111). A kiro `.jsonl`'s first line is `{"version":"v1","kind":"Prompt",...}` (confirmed in the smoke transcript) — no `type:session` header, no `parentSession` field — so this never matches.
3. A parser that sets `ev.ParentSessionID` (consumed at `session_detector_activity.go`). The kiro parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go`) never sets `ParentSessionID`; the entire kirocli package has zero parent/child/subagent/spawn references (grep-confirmed).

Decisively, the parent link kiro DOES persist lives ONLY in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar (`loop_id.agent_id.parent_id` and `session_created_reason`), NOT in the `<uuid>.jsonl` the daemon tails — a grep of the smoke `.jsonl` for any parent/session_meta/parentSession reference returns empty. The kiro adapter reads the sidecar only for the working directory (`GetCWDFromTranscript` → top-level `cwd`), not for parent linkage. So the daemon never sees a kiro subagent as a *linked child session* — the core observable this scenario asserts.

This is not a narrow `bug` (a spec-required observable mis-handled on a trace the daemon already reads) — there is no kiro parent-linking code path at all, and the only on-disk source of the link (the sidecar) is outside what the JSONL Source tails. It is a missing capability → `incapable`. Building it is new adapter work: read `loop_id.agent_id.parent_id` (and gate on `session_created_reason == "subagent"`) out of the `.json` sidecar and surface it as `ParentSessionID`.

## Why frozen

With `agent=yes` and `daemon=incapable`, the route is `frozen` per the routing table. As a corollary, even the recipe's `sigkill` step is a stubbed driver arm for kiro (`DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"`), but that is moot under `frozen` — no recording runs, so `driver=n/a` (matching the codex cell). Re-assess once the kiro adapter learns to read the sidecar parent link; the orphan-sweep / no-working-transition invariant (#321) is daemon-internal and agent-agnostic, already covered by the claudecode and opencode cells, so the kiro-specific blocker is strictly the parent-child link.
caveats: Calibrated against the codex subagent-orphan-cleanup cell (agent=yes, daemon=incapable, frozen, applicable_false): same structural blocker — the parent->child link exists in agent-internal metadata the adapter's JSONL Source does not read.

Unblock path: extend the kiro adapter (or GetCWDFromTranscript's sidecar read) to also pull session_state.conversation_metadata...loop_id.agent_id.parent_id from the <uuid>.json sidecar, gated on session_created_reason == 'subagent', and surface it as event.ParentSessionID. That alone flips daemon -> full and unfreezes this cell; the JSONL transport needs no change.

Provisional emission: the live smoke session was a top-level chat (its sidecar's parent_id is None despite session_created_reason 'subagent'); no genuine spawned child rollout was captured, so the exact populated value of loop_id.agent_id.parent_id in a real subagent's sidecar is inferred from the schema + the 'Record parent session ID in persisted subagent sessions' changelog, not from an observed child sidecar.
The sigkill driver step is a stubbed arm for kiro (not in DRIVE_ELICITS); it would be a driver gap to port IF the cell were ever recordable, but under frozen no recording runs so driver=n/a.

sources.note: Kiro parser never sets event.ParentSessionID; the entire kirocli package has zero parent/child/subagent/spawn linking references (grep-confirmed).
Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli} tailing flat <uuid>.jsonl files (no subagents/ subdir); the adapter reads the .json sidecar only for cwd, never for parent linkage.

deriveParentSession (line 90): deriveParentSessionID (line 100) matches only .../subagents/<child>.jsonl; deriveParentSessionFromTranscript (line 111) matches only a {type:session, parentSession:...} header. A kiro flat path + {version:v1, kind:Prompt} first line match neither.

Live 2.5.1 sidecar: top-level session_created_reason='subagent'; session_state.conversation_metadata.user_turn_metadatas[].loop_id.agent_id.parent_id present (None in this top-level session). The parent link is recorded ONLY here, not in the .jsonl the daemon tails.

Live 2.5.1 transcript: first line {version:v1, kind:Prompt, ...}; grep for parentSession/parent_session/session_meta/type:session/created_reason returns empty — the JSONL carries NO parent reference and no model/token/cost.

InvokeSubagents tool ('Array of subagent invocations to execute in parallel'); /spawn background sessions; changelog 'Introducing Subagents...' and 'Record parent session ID in persisted subagent sessions' — agent spawns AND persists the parent link to disk.

kiro-cli 2.5.1 live smoke (§4, §8): JSONL event vocab Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear with NO model/usage/cost; slash surface includes spawn; sidecar <uuid>.json carries per-turn metering/model but the parser does not read it for linkage.
Zombie-on-restart fix establishing the no-working-transition-during-cleanup invariant; agent-agnostic, already covered by claudecode + opencode cells — not the kiro blocker.

recipe.note: Frozen: agent=yes but daemon=incapable. No recipe runs because the daemon cannot observe a kiro-cli subagent as a linked child session (no kiro parent-child linking path; the parent_id lives only in the .json sidecar the JSONL Source never reads). Recipe would otherwise mirror claudecode's 3-4 cell (use InvokeSubagents/spawn a subagent that reads a file and waits; SIGKILL the process group; observe the sweep) AND would need the stubbed sigkill driver arm ported. Re-author once kiro parent-linking lands in the adapter.
